root_text.an11.4:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an11.4:1.3: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato sāriputtassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an11.4:2.2: avippaṭisāre asati avippaṭisāravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:2.3: pāmojje asati pāmojjavipannassa hatūpanisā hoti pīti,
root_text.an11.4:2.4: pītiyā asati pītivipannassa hatūpanisā hoti passaddhi,
root_text.an11.4:2.5: passaddhiyā asati passaddhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti sukhaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:2.6: sukhe asati sukhavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti sammāsamādhi,
root_text.an11.4:2.7: sammāsamādhimhi asati sammāsamādhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:2.8: yathābhūtañāṇadassane asati yathābhūtañāṇadassanavipannassa hatūpanisā hoti nibbidā,
root_text.an11.4:2.9: nibbidāya asati nibbidāvipannassa hatūpaniso hoti virāgo,
root_text.an11.4:2.10: virāge asati virāgavipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an11.4:3.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, rukkho sākhāpalāsavipanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi na pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an11.4:3.2: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, dussīlassa sīlavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti avippaṭisāro,
root_text.an11.4:3.3: avippaṭisāre asati avippaṭisāravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ …pe…
root_text.an11.4:4.1: Sīlavato, āvuso, sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro,
root_text.an11.4:4.2: avippaṭisāre sati avippaṭisārasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:4.3: pāmojje sati pāmojjasampannassa upanisasampannā hoti pīti,
root_text.an11.4:4.4: pītiyā sati pītisampannassa upanisasampannā hoti passaddhi,
root_text.an11.4:4.5: passaddhiyā sati passaddhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti sukhaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:4.6: sukhe sati sukhasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti sammāsamādhi,
root_text.an11.4:4.7: sammāsamādhimhi sati sammāsamādhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ,
root_text.an11.4:4.8: yathābhūtañāṇadassane sati yathābhūtañāṇadassanasampannassa upanisasampannā hoti nibbidā,
root_text.an11.4:4.9: nibbidāya sati nibbidāsampannassa upanisasampanno hoti virāgo,
root_text.an11.4:4.10: virāge sati virāgasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an11.4:5.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, rukkho sākhāpalāsasampanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an11.4:5.2: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, sīlavato sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro,
root_text.an11.4:5.3: avippaṭisāre sati avippaṭisārasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ …pe…

translation_text.an11.4:1.1: There Venerable Sāriputta addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an11.4:2.1: “An unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an11.4:2.2: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an11.4:2.3: When there is no joy, one who lacks joy has destroyed a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an11.4:2.4: When there is no rapture, one who lacks rapture has destroyed a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an11.4:2.5: When there is no tranquility, one who lacks tranquility has destroyed a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an11.4:2.6: When there is no bliss, one who lacks bliss has destroyed a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an11.4:2.7: When there is no right immersion, one who lacks right immersion has destroyed a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an11.4:2.8: When there is no true knowledge and vision, one who lacks true knowledge and vision has destroyed a vital condition for disillusionment.
translation_text.an11.4:2.9: When there is no disillusionment, one who lacks disillusionment has destroyed a vital condition for dispassion.
translation_text.an11.4:2.10: When there is no dispassion, one who lacks dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.4:3.1: Suppose there was a tree that lacked branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would not grow to fullness.
translation_text.an11.4:3.2: In the same way, an unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for having no regrets.
translation_text.an11.4:3.3: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an11.4:3.4: When there is dispassion, one who lacks dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.4:4.1: An ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an11.4:4.2: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an11.4:4.3: When there is joy, one who has fulfilled joy has fulfilled a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an11.4:4.4: When there is rapture, one who has fulfilled rapture has fulfilled a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an11.4:4.5: When there is tranquility, one who has fulfilled tranquility has fulfilled a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an11.4:4.6: When there is bliss, one who has fulfilled bliss has fulfilled a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an11.4:4.7: When there is right immersion, one who has fulfilled right immersion has fulfilled a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an11.4:4.8: When there is true knowledge and vision, one who has fulfilled true knowledge and vision has fulfilled a vital condition for disillusionment.
translation_text.an11.4:4.9: When there is disillusionment, one who has fulfilled disillusionment has fulfilled a vital condition for dispassion.
translation_text.an11.4:4.10: When there is dispassion, one who has fulfilled dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an11.4:5.1: Suppose there was a tree that was complete with branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would grow to fullness.
translation_text.an11.4:5.2: In the same way, an ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an11.4:5.3: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an11.4:5.4: When there is dispassion, one who has fulfilled dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.”
